sections.paragraph:
(“trembling,” perhaps “earthquake”), [Maralah], pr.n. of a town in the tribe of Zebulun, Jos 19:11.

I. מַרְפֵּא m. and מַרְפֵּה Jer 8:15 (from the root רָפָא to heal).-

(1) the healing (of a disease), 2Ch 21:18 36:16 Jer 14:19.-Hence-

(2) refreshing, both of the body, Pro 4:22 16:24 and of the mind, Pro 12:18 13:17 .

(3) deliverance (from calamity), Pro 6:15 29:1 Mal. 3:20 .

(4) remedy, Jer 33:6.

II. מַרְפֵּא (from the root רָפָא=רָפָה to relax), prop. relaxed mind; hence tranquillity of mind, meekness; Pro 14:30, לֵב מַרְפֵּא “a meek heart;” Pro 15:4 מַרְפֵּא לָשֹׁון “tranquillity of tongue,” i.e. gentle, modest speech; Ecc 10:4, “gentleness hinders great offences.”
